title: Mailchimp vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and Substack: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
intro: Mailchimp and Substack are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different purposes. Mailchimp is a comprehensive marketing automation suite designed for businesses and ecommerce, while Substack is a newsletter platform built for writers and creators to monetize directly from subscribers. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Freemium (free up to 500 contacts, paid plans from $13/month)
comparison_table.tool_b: Revenue-share (free forever, Substack takes 10% of paid subscriptions)

comparison_table.tool_a: 500 on free tier; unlimited on paid plans
Automation, A/B testing, landing pages, audience segmentation, analytics
comparison_table.tool_b: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, built-in audience, no limits, notes feed

comparison_table.tool_a: Small businesses, ecommerce, marketing campaigns, lead generation
comparison_table.tool_b: Writers, podcasters, creators monetizing content directly

choose_a_if: You need advanced marketing automation and A/B testing capabilities
You run an ecommerce business or manage multiple marketing campaigns
You want to build landing pages and segment audiences for targeted outreach
choose_b_if: You're a writer or creator looking to monetize a newsletter directly
You want unlimited subscribers with no scaling costs
You plan to host podcasts or build a community around your content

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Mailchimp is better for businesses needing marketing automation, landing pages, and advanced analytics. Substack is better for writers and creators focused on monetizing newsletters directly. For general email marketing to businesses, Mailchimp has the edge; for content creators, Substack is superior.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or Substack?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper for paid newsletters since it charges only a 10% revenue share on subscriber payments and is completely free otherwise. Mailchimp is cheaper for businesses with small contact lists under 500, but becomes more expensive as you scale. For unlimited growth, Substack's revenue-share model offers better long-term value.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, most email platforms allow you to export your subscriber list. You can export your Substack subscribers and import them into Mailchimp. However, you'll lose Substack-specific features like paid subscriptions and podcast hosting, and you'll need to redirect subscribers to your new Mailchimp newsletter.
